title: The Italian Bob

jokes.text: Enjoy your multicultural hooey.
jokes.setup: Teacher introduces Willie to present diversity tales as educational content
jokes.punchline: Willie immediately dismisses it with characteristic bluntness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment - his contempt for educational material expressed with his signature gruffness

jokes.text: What is this crap? This is worse than 'wheelchair-nocchio.'
jokes.setup: Watching terrible educational movie about tolerance
jokes.punchline: References fictional bad adaptation combining Pinocchio with disability themes
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that perfectly captures the ham-fisted nature of preachy children's entertainment

jokes.text: Hey, cryptkeeper, I like your Dodge scare-a-van.
jokes.setup: Nelson sees Mr. Burns's old car
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Caravan' with horror references fitting the decrepit vehicle
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay that captures both the car's age and Nelson's bullying nature

jokes.text: How dare you mock my mobile-a-mobile? It was the first car to outrun a man. A caveman.
jokes.setup: Burns defends his ancient car's capabilities
jokes.punchline: Clarification that it only outran a caveman reveals how truly old the car is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - his extreme age and disconnect from modernity delivered with characteristic pomposity

jokes.text: I thought of that 'cause I slept in a cave last night.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions caveman
jokes.punchline: Cletus casually reveals he literally slept in a cave
jokes.explanation: Classic Cletus - his extreme poverty and primitive living conditions delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Kill them, Smithers. / You could just buy a new car, sir. / Well... whatever's easier.
jokes.setup: Burns orders violence against children who mocked his car
jokes.punchline: Considers murder and car shopping as equivalent options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns characterization - his casual attitude toward violence versus mundane tasks

jokes.text: May I suggest an Oldsmobile?
jokes.setup: Burns needs a new car to replace his ancient one
jokes.punchline: Smithers suggests a brand literally named 'Old'
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay that fits Smithers' helpful but slightly sarcastic nature

jokes.text: I won't forget who mocked me. I'm taking your picture. Now, hold perfectly still for 78 minutes.
jokes.setup: Burns wants to document who mocked him
jokes.punchline: Using extremely old camera technology requiring impossibly long exposure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns gag - his extreme age manifesting in hilariously outdated technology

jokes.text: That's the car I think about when I make love to my wife.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions the Lamborgotti Fasterossa
jokes.punchline: Smithers reveals inappropriate sexual association with the car
jokes.explanation: Classic Smithers TMI moment - his repressed sexuality manifesting inappropriately

jokes.text: That guy in first class is taking a tv out of his armrest. / What's in you? / A bunch of stupid cables.
jokes.setup: Bart observes first-class luxury
jokes.punchline: Homer's economy seat only contains useless wiring
jokes.explanation: Standard airline class disparity joke - functional but not particularly original

jokes.text: The kids just got a new playhouse! / Cletus, you are the most wonderful husband and son I ever had.
jokes.setup: Cletus shows Brandine the airplane
jokes.punchline: Brandine reveals their incestuous relationship casually
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about hillbilly stereotypes delivered with perfect casual tone

jokes.text: Well, some people in Europe have the impression that America has made some stupid choices in the past, oh, five years.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about Canadian flag on backpack
jokes.punchline: Lisa diplomatically references Bush administration foreign policy
jokes.explanation: Smart political satire delivered with Lisa's characteristic intelligence and tact

jokes.text: That flag is mine! Don't mess with Texas! Shock and awe, losers! Shock and awe!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to maintain Canadian disguise
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately blows cover with aggressive American patriotism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely undermining Lisa's plan with oblivious patriotism

jokes.text: Why you little...
jokes.setup: Lisa makes sarcastic comment about health insurance
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature throttling response
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to Homer's signature move - relies on familiarity but effective

jokes.text: Wow, I've seen photos of this, but you can't really experience it until you're here: A Mcdonald's where you can get booze!
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing Italy for the first time
jokes.punchline: Focuses on McDonald's alcohol instead of historic architecture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - ignoring culture for fast food and alcohol

jokes.text: But, dad, don't you want to turn around and see the tower? / There's a picture of it on my cup.
jokes.setup: Lisa encourages Homer to see Leaning Tower of Pisa
jokes.punchline: Homer satisfied with McDonald's cup image
jokes.explanation: Great commentary on American tourist behavior and Homer's low cultural standards

jokes.text: Savages.
jokes.setup: Learning about Pompeii victims frozen in volcanic ash
jokes.punchline: Homer calls the victims savages for their frozen positions
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's complete misunderstanding of tragic historical event

jokes.text: It says here we should turn left at a fat chick in a tutu being fed by a midget. / That's a dvd of a Fellini movie.
jokes.setup: Homer reads navigation system for directions
jokes.punchline: He's describing a Fellini film as if it's a real landmark
jokes.explanation: Brilliant confusion between art film and reality, showcasing Homer's cultural ignorance

jokes.text: I get it. The midget represents dwarves.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains it's a Fellini movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong 'deep' interpretation
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at film analysis shows his complete misunderstanding of symbolism

jokes.text: Mozzarella! Parmigiano! Provolone! Pecorino! Gorgonzola! Fontina! Taleggio! Bocconcini!
jokes.setup: Homer crashes into cheese truck
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire listing of cheese varieties with increasing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy enhanced by Homer's food obsession, though somewhat repetitive

jokes.text: Don't worry. We got the cheese insurance. / It doesn't cover mortadella. / No!
jokes.setup: Homer reassures about insurance coverage
jokes.punchline: Specific exclusion for the exact item they damaged
jokes.explanation: Classic insurance comedy - comprehensive coverage with specific devastating exclusion

jokes.text: Stupid Italy. Wish you'd never been unified by Victor Emmanuel II. If only you'd stayed a loose confederation of city-states, trading with each other and occasionally warring.
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated with car accident
jokes.punchline: Specific historical knowledge about Italian unification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Homer displaying unexpected historical knowledge in moment of anger

jokes.text: Americano? / What the hell could that mean? Why can't you people learn to speak my language? I learned to eat your food.
jokes.setup: Mechanic identifies Homer as American
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious entitlement about language while abroad
jokes.explanation: Perfect ugly American stereotype - demanding others accommodate his language barriers

jokes.text: kentucky?! / In italia, this a-means 'whore'!
jokes.setup: Homer wearing Kentucky shirt introduced to Italian crowd
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate translation creates embarrassing misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Language barrier humor with appropriately embarrassing cultural misunderstanding

jokes.text: Sideshow Bob of all the regioni and all the villaggi in all of Italia, you had to be il mayore of this one!
jokes.setup: Simpsons discover Sideshow Bob is the mayor
jokes.punchline: Parody of Casablanca's famous line adapted to Italian setting
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of classic movie quote with Italian vocabulary integration

jokes.text: But it better have a beginning, a middle and an end. And you'd better make us root for the protagonist.
jokes.setup: Bob about to tell his backstory
jokes.punchline: Lisa demands proper narrative structure
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on storytelling conventions delivered with Lisa's characteristic intelligence

jokes.text: Okay, so far I'm rooting for you.
jokes.setup: Bob mentions attempting to murder Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer expresses support for the murder attempt
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's occasional antagonism toward Bart

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'buon giorno.' Your mouth is getting lazy on the second syllable. Here, let me guide you.
jokes.setup: Bob learning Italian from locals
jokes.punchline: Bob's pretentious correction of pronunciation coaching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob characterization - his intellectual arrogance even as a student

jokes.text: These feet are made for stomping and that's just what they'll do one of these days these feet will make chianti out of you ready, feet? Start stomping.
jokes.setup: Bob grape stomping in Italian vineyard
jokes.punchline: Parody of Nancy Sinatra song adapted to winemaking
jokes.explanation: Clever song parody that fits the grape stomping activity, though slightly forced

jokes.text: Holy moley! I always thought you were, you know, out loud and proud. / Well, I experimented in college, as one does.
jokes.setup: Bob reveals he has a wife
jokes.punchline: Bob casually dismisses homosexuality as college experimentation
jokes.explanation: Commentary on assumptions about Bob's sexuality with his characteristic pretension

jokes.text: I never went to college. / Stop the presses.
jokes.setup: Homer admits he never went to college
jokes.punchline: Bob's sarcastic mock-surprise at obvious information
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob condescension toward Homer's lack of education

jokes.text: The name Roberto cries when he has the bad dream. Bart Simpson! Bart Simpson! Bart Simpson! I make like my daddy!
jokes.setup: Bob's wife introduces their son to Bart
jokes.punchline: Child mimics father's murderous tendencies toward Bart
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inherited murderous impulses delivered through innocent child

jokes.text: He makes love like a man who just got out of jail.
jokes.setup: Wife praising Bob to the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual revelation referencing Bob's prison time
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor combining sexual TMI with reference to Bob's criminal past

jokes.text: Surely, even the most heinous criminal deserves a seventh chance.
jokes.setup: Bob pleading for forgiveness from Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Specific count reveals his numerous previous failed redemptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Bob's history of failed reform through specific number

jokes.text: Yeah, I tell Marge everything. Not necessarily in words, but in body language. You know, sneaking around and such.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to tell spouse about past misdeeds
jokes.punchline: Homer's definition of 'telling' includes suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic about marital communication and transparency

jokes.text: You can't be a bad person if you have a family. And literature is filled with tales of redemption from Jean Valjean to the voice of Buzz Lightyear, Tim Allen.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Bob's potential for redemption
jokes.punchline: Comparing classic literature to Tim Allen's minor scandals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of high literature with celebrity drug bust - perfect Lisa intelligence

jokes.text: Dad, don't act like Mussolini. / I thought I was doing Donald Trump.
jokes.setup: Homer making grandiose gestures to Italian crowd
jokes.punchline: Homer confuses fascist dictator impression with businessman
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary comparing authoritarian gestures with prescient Trump reference

jokes.text: This sausage shop has been grinding the same family of pigs for 600 years.
jokes.setup: Bob giving tour of local sausage shop
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous phrasing suggests either pig lineage or generational murder
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay that works on multiple levels - innocent farming vs. sinister interpretation

jokes.text: Chief, I tried to warn you. Sideshow bob hasn't lived here for months. / Oh, god, am I sorry. Is that a penn state banner? 'Cause my cousin went there. Did you know him? Mark Wiggum, fat kid, played a lot of tetris.
jokes.setup: Wiggum raids wrong location looking for Bob
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to personal college connection
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum non-sequitur showing his complete inability to focus on police work

jokes.text: Tomorrow, they return to America, taking with them my gratitude, my friendship and my heartfelt wish that they never return.
jokes.setup: Bob making farewell speech about the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Polite gratitude undermined by honest desire to never see them again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob eloquence masking his true feelings with diplomatic language

jokes.text: Look, the town drunk is two years old.
jokes.setup: Marge objects to giving Lisa wine
jokes.punchline: Homer points out extremely young alcoholic as normal in Italy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cultural drinking differences taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Yeah, but he's a wanted killer in our community. / He deserves to be hailed at this wingding. / More like jailed at sing sing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bob's status in two communities
jokes.punchline: Lisa's wordplay contrasting celebration vs. imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay showing Lisa's wit, though somewhat forced rhyming

jokes.text: Drunken children tell the ugliest lies.
jokes.setup: Lisa reveals Bob's murderous past while intoxicated
jokes.punchline: Bob dismisses truth as drunken lies
jokes.explanation: Bob's attempt to discredit truth using alcohol as excuse shows his manipulation skills

jokes.text: But darling, the boy drove me to murder by exonerating a harlequin who I framed for robbery at the Kwik-E-Mart. / It all sounds so stupid.
jokes.setup: Bob explains his motivation for attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Wife's reaction acknowledges the absurdity of cartoon logic
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on how ridiculous cartoon villain motivations sound in reality

jokes.text: It all sounds so stupid.
jokes.setup: Bob gives elaborate justification for attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Wife's simple, devastating dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Bob's pretentious explanation. The economy and understatement make it devastating.

jokes.text: Boy, that Sideshow Bob goes from zero to murder awfully fast. / At least this time it was Lisa who ruined Bob's life.
jokes.setup: Bob immediately turns violent after being exposed
jokes.punchline: Bart notes Lisa caused the trouble instead of him
jokes.explanation: Meta-observation about Bob's predictable pattern plus sibling dynamics

jokes.text: It's called a hangover, sweetie. And it's an unavoidable part of life.
jokes.setup: Lisa feels sick after drinking wine
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact parenting about alcohol consequences
jokes.explanation: Marge's resigned acceptance of childhood hangovers as inevitable shows warped priorities

jokes.text: What am i supposed to use it for... transporting water to distant urban centers?! Lazy romans.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out they're driving on ancient Roman aqueduct
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically dismisses the original engineering purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic dismissing one of history's greatest engineering achievements

jokes.text: I feel like I'm the Bourne Identity.
jokes.setup: Family being chased through foreign city
jokes.punchline: Marge compares situation to action thriller
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference that fits situation but lacks originality

jokes.text: I am the gayest super villain ever. Beware my scented candles.
jokes.setup: Homer does Phantom of the Opera impression
jokes.punchline: Combines gay stereotype with non-threatening villain accessories
jokes.explanation: Homer's confused character impression relies somewhat on stereotypes

jokes.text: I don't wish to brag, but he's evil at an eighth grade level.
jokes.setup: Observing Krusty's performance
jokes.punchline: Academic assessment of comedy quality using education metrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob intellectualism applied to lowbrow entertainment with academic precision

jokes.text: I know you're out there. I can hear you being greasy.
jokes.setup: Krusty addressing Italian audience during performance
jokes.punchline: Insulting audience with stereotype about Italian greasiness
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty offensiveness toward audience, though relies on ethnic stereotype

jokes.text: Well, I need someone to help me smuggle antiquities to America. Everyone, break off a piece and hide it on your person.
jokes.setup: Krusty rescues the Simpsons from Bob
jokes.punchline: Reveals his rescue was motivated by need for smuggling accomplices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - apparent heroism undermined by criminal self-interest

jokes.text: When a big tub of lard... / Knock it off. / Excusi. No speaka english.
jokes.setup: Gondolier singing insulting song about Homer in English
jokes.punchline: Claims not to speak English when confronted
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff showing the gondolier's deliberate insult disguised as language barrier

tmdb_overview: Homer brings the whole family along when he is sent to Italy to pick up a new sports car for Mr. Burns, when they find themselves in a small town that Sideshow Bob has become the mayor of.
